messages.content: How many credits does Webb require for graduation?
Webb requires a minimum of 20 credits for graduation. [source: course catalog]
How many classes are Webb students required to take each semester?
Students are required to take five courses each semester. A sixth is optional for many students, but seven courses are not permitted. [source: course catalog]
What is the deadline for new students to complete course selection in SIS?
The catalog says new students must complete course selection in SIS by May 1. [source: course catalog]
Who should students contact about transcripts or records?
The registrar handles transcripts and school records. Webb lists Briar Rose Tirpak as registrar. [source: handbook] [source: directory]
When did Webb stop offering AP courses?
The course catalog says Webb formally discontinued AP courses in fall 2025 and replaced them with Advanced Studies courses. [source: course catalog]
If a student takes an AP exam off campus, will Webb excuse them from classes or activities?
No. The catalog says students who choose to take AP exams off campus will not be excused from Webb academic or extracurricular obligations. [source: course catalog]
Do all Webb students have to do an afternoon activity every season?
Yes. The course catalog says all students participate in the afternoon program each season of the year. [source: course catalog]
Does Webb require students to do an interscholastic sport at least once a year?
Yes. Webb requires all students to participate in an interscholastic sport for at least one season each year. [source: course catalog]
Can Sports Broadcasting count toward team sport credit?
Yes, for juniors or seniors. The catalog says juniors or seniors can earn team sport credit through Sports Broadcasting. [source: course catalog]
Does winter Dance Company count as sports credit?
Yes. The catalog says the winter dance program counts as a sports credit because of its physical demands and culminating performance. [source: course catalog]
Do you need experience to join Paleontology Museum Lab?
No prior experience is required. The catalog says student volunteers can help with things like fossil cleaning, laser scanning, and exhibit maintenance. [source: course catalog]
What is Science Fair Research Lab actually for?
It gives students a place to develop independent scientific research and prepare for science fairs with support in research design, data generation, and presentation. [source: course catalog]
Can beginners take Art Studio in the spring?
Yes. The catalog says prior visual arts experience is not required for Art Studio. [source: course catalog]
What kinds of things can students do in winter Music Performance?

Depending on skill level and instrumentation, students may do coached private practice, small ensemble work, jazz combo work, individualized study, or a self-designed music project. [source: course catalog]
Does Webb's triathlon program require students to own a bike?
Students need access to a bicycle. The catalog also says they must provide running shoes and a bathing suit. [source: course catalog]
Where do Webb golf teams practice?
The catalog says the golf teams practice at Marshall Canyon. [source: course catalog]
For football, what gear do students have to provide themselves?
Players must provide cleats. The catalog says all other equipment is furnished by Webb. [source: course catalog]
What is special about Tennis Clinic compared with the regular team season?
Tennis Clinic is the winter off-season program. The catalog says it focuses on skill development and includes ladder-style tournaments. [source: course catalog]
If a day student is coming to community dinner, when do they have to notify the school?
By 10 a.m. that day. The handbook says day students should notify the student accountability coordinator no later than 10 a.m. on the day of community dinner. [source: handbook]
Can students order DoorDash during the school week?
No. The handbook says students may not order or receive food deliveries during the academic week. [source: handbook]
What time do day students have to leave campus if they stay for evening study hours?
By 9 p.m. The handbook says day students who stay on campus during study hours must sign out and depart by then. [source: handbook]
What happens to missed in-class work after an unexcused absence?
The handbook says missed in-class graded work such as tests, quizzes, or written assignments receives a failing grade. [source: handbook]
When can Webb hold a hearing for possible loss of credit because of absences?
A hearing may be held when a student records five absences in the same course in one semester. [source: handbook]
Can parents see their child's school records at Webb?
Yes. The handbook says parents have the right to inspect their child's school records through the registrar. [source: handbook]
I can handle six classes on paper, but how should I decide if I actually should take six?
Look at the whole year, not just your ability in isolation. A sixth class makes sense when it adds meaningful value and still leaves room for steady performance, sleep, activities, and recovery.
How should I think about being recommended into multiple Honors or Advanced Studies classes at once?

A recommendation means the department thinks you could succeed there. It does not mean you need to take every advanced option at once. The better question is which combination makes your year stronger and more manageable.
How should someone compare Honors Integrated Math II with standard Integrated Math II?

The honors option usually makes sense if the student is ready for more depth and pace without compromising the rest of the schedule. The standard course can still be the right choice if it supports stronger understanding and steadier performance.
How should I compare Art AB and Media Arts as a first art course?

Art AB is a stronger fit if you want an introductory visual art foundation through materials, making, and creative process. Media Arts is better if you are more drawn to digital illustration, animation, game design, or other technology-based creative work.
How should a student compare Debate and Model UN?

Debate is usually better if you want sharper argument, refutation, and fast-response reasoning. Model UN is better if you want structured public speaking, diplomacy, and policy discussion in a more representative format.
What is the smartest way to use office hours?
Use them before a problem becomes large. They work best when you come in with a specific question, a draft, or a clear point of confusion rather than waiting until everything has piled up.
What does workload 1.0 versus 1.25 actually tell me?
It is a signal about expected time and intensity. A 1.25 course should usually be treated as heavier, so it matters when you are deciding what else to stack around it.
How should I compare Paleontology Museum Lab with a more traditional science activity?

Paleontology Museum Lab is better if you want hands-on museum and fossil work in a research-centered environment. A more traditional activity may fit better if you want broader science exploration without that specific museum focus.
How should someone choose between Robotics and Technical Theater?

Choose Robotics if you want engineering, programming, iterative design, and competition. Choose Technical Theater if you want backstage production, lighting, sound, construction, and the craft of making performances work.
Why does Webb tell students to think about non-academic commitments when choosing classes?
Because a schedule is not just a list of courses. Leadership, service, athletics, arts, and daily life all take real time and energy, so the academic plan has to fit the actual student.
How should I compare Advanced Art with New Media Culture & Communication?

Advanced Art is stronger for students who want deeper work in visual art practice and developing an artistic voice through physical and mixed media. New Media Culture & Communication is stronger for students who want advanced digital tools, independent media projects, and an online portfolio path.
How should someone compare Advanced Studies Statistics and Honors Number Theory?

Advanced Studies Statistics is the better fit if you want applied quantitative reasoning and data-centered thinking. Honors Number Theory is better if you enjoy more abstract mathematical structure and problem solving.
How should I think about whether I am actually ready for Independent Study?

A real Independent Study is usually for a student who has already exhausted normal offerings in that area and can propose a serious academic plan with supervision. It should look like disciplined study, not just personal interest.
How should a student think about a program away from Webb in junior year?

Treat it as a major academic decision, not just an interesting side opportunity. It should have a clear purpose, fit graduation and college planning, and be strong enough to justify leaving the normal Webb path for a time.
What is the practical difference between dropping a course before the deadline and after it?
Before the deadline, the dropped course does not appear on the transcript. After the deadline, it may show as WP or WF, so the timing matters.
How should I think about required office hours if they get assigned?
Treat them as structured support, not as a verdict on your ability. The point is to create consistency before the course gets harder to recover in.
How should someone compare Cross Country and Functional Fitness?

Cross Country is the better fit if you want endurance training, racing, and distance-running identity. Functional Fitness is better if you want broader strength and conditioning without centering your season around running competition.
How should I compare Musical Theater and the fall Theater program?

Musical Theater is better if you want singing, dance, and movement as core parts of performance. The fall Theater program is better if you want acting and script-based performance without the musical format being central.
How do I choose between a course I genuinely like and one that just sounds stronger?
Start with where you are more likely to do meaningful work. A course chosen for substance usually ages better than one chosen only for appearance.
Did my course change request get approved yet?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. That would need to come from the registrar, your advisor, or the official schedule update.
Who is my dorm head next year?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. That is a student-specific residential assignment that would need to come from the school.
What is Prom's theme this year?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. That kind of year-specific event detail should come from the current student announcement or calendar.
Who won Jubilee last year?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. I would want an official school source rather than guessing.
Is AI Club actually meeting this week?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. The club list shows that the club exists, but not its current weekly meeting status. [source: clubs and affinity groups]
What room is chapel in today?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. The chapel materials describe the program, but today's location or setup would need the current schedule. [source: chapel program]
Is one of the math teachers leaving after this semester?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. I would not want to guess about staffing changes.
Did the track meet get canceled because of weather?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. That is a live athletics detail that should come from the current team communication.
Was my late lights request approved for tonight?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. That would have to come from residential staff or the relevant nightly approval process.
How many students are in AdvSt Chemistry next year?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Enrollment numbers can change, so I would check the registrar or official schedule information.
Is dinner tonight taco night?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. The dining page gives examples of themed meals but not tonight's exact menu. [source: dining]
Is the next LA weekend trip already full?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Weekend trip capacity is a live signup detail. [source: after school and weekends]
Did my SAT registration at Webb go through?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. SAT registration is managed through the College Board, so I would check the actual registration record. [source: handbook]
Which club is considered the most prestigious?
I can't confirm that from the information I have, and that kind of ranking is subjective anyway. If you want, I can help compare clubs for a specific goal.
Is a Northwestern rep coming to campus next week?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. That would depend on the current college guidance calendar, not the general school materials.
Can I still do it?
What is the 'it' you mean here: a course change, an appeal, an activity signup, or something else?
Is that enough?
Enough for what goal?
What should I ask for?
What situation are you dealing with: a class issue, an extension, an accommodation, a meeting, or something else?
Can you fix this?
Yes. Paste the sentence, email, or plan you want fixed.
What rule applies here?
What is the situation?
Should I be worried about this grade?
What grade is it, in what class, and are you more worried about course fit, GPA, or promotion to the next level?
Can you explain what they meant?
Yes. Paste what they said, and I will translate it into plain language.
How should I respond?
Respond to what exactly: an email, a teacher comment, a policy decision, or a text?
What should I change next year?
What does your current schedule or activity load look like now?
Can I count that?
Count it toward what: graduation credit, a requirement, an activity, or something else?
Should I tell them now or wait?
Tell them what?
Can you compare my options?
Yes. What are the options, and what matters most to you in the choice?
How do I bring it up?
What conversation are you trying to have?
Can you help me make a plan?
Yes. What kind of plan do you need: academic, scheduling, email, or something else?
Thanks, that clears it up.
Glad it helped.
I bombed one quiz and now I feel like the whole course is doomed.
One bad quiz can matter, but it does not automatically define the course. The next step is usually to figure out whether the issue was understanding, preparation, or adjustment to the class.
Write me a fake reason for why I missed study hall.
I can't help with a fake reason. I can help you write a direct and honest explanation instead.
Can you make this email subject line better: need help asap
A cleaner version is: Request for help with [course or issue].
How do I make this sound more direct: I was kind of wondering if maybe we could talk sometime?
A more direct version is: Could we talk sometime this week?
How do I politely say no to one more commitment?
You could say: Thank you for thinking of me, but I need to protect my current workload, so I should pass.
Can you summarize this in one line: I am trying to decide whether this class is still a good fit for my overall year.
I am trying to assess whether this class still fits my overall schedule and goals.
What is a calm way to say I forgot to submit the form?
You could say: I realized I did not submit the form when I should have, and I wanted to acknowledge that directly.
Can you help me ask a teacher for clarification without sounding lost?
Yes. A simple version is: I wanted to check that I am understanding the assignment correctly. Could I ask one or two clarifying questions?
How do I say I appreciate the update?
You can say: Thank you for the update. I appreciate you letting me know.
I feel embarrassed asking a basic question.
Basic questions are often the ones that save people the most trouble later. Asking early is usually a strength.
Can you give me a short apology for missing a message?
Sorry I missed your message earlier.
Make this less sharp: You never told me any of this.
A calmer version is: I do not think I understood this earlier, and I wanted to follow up now.
I am overwhelmed and do not even know where to start.
Start with the task that has the nearest real consequence. Then do only the first piece of it, not the whole week in your head.
I am nervous to go to office hours because I do not want to look weak.
Going to office hours usually looks responsible, not weak. It shows that you are trying to deal with the problem early.
I want a schedule that looks ambitious, but I also know I burn out when every class is heavy.
Then your goal is not maximum heaviness. It is credible ambition.
What does that mean in practice?
It means choosing the strongest program you can still carry well, not the one that sounds most extreme.
Use school policy directly when it is confirmed in the handbook or catalog. Do not guess beyond it.
Can a student add a class because they want a different free block?

Not as a normal basis for a change. The catalog says requests for a free block will not be granted, and the handbook says those requests are not likely to be granted. [source: course catalog] [source: handbook]
im tryna pick between Robotics, Sci Fair Lab, and Debate but also i lowkey want the one that sounds best for college
Start by separating two questions: which one fits you best, and which one sounds strongest from a distance.
Okay so how would you compare them?

Robotics is strongest for team engineering and build-program-compete work. Science Fair Research Lab is strongest for independent research and formal project development. Debate is strongest for speaking, argument, and fast reasoning. The best choice depends on what kind of work you actually want to do repeatedly.
What is the least work option that still looks the most impressive?
That is usually the wrong way to frame it. The stronger question is which option gives you the best learning and strongest work without pushing your schedule past what you can sustain.
I missed class without a good reason and now I need to email my teacher, but I do not want to sound fake.
Then keep it brief and accountable.
Can you draft it?
Hi [Name], I missed class and that was my responsibility. I understand that this creates a problem, and I wanted to ask what I should do next. Best, [Your Name]
When the user asks about add/drop timing, answer from the confirmed materials and do not merge conflicting rules without noting them.
How many academic days do students have to add or drop a class?

The handbook says the first ten academic days of each semester. The course catalog says the first seven academic days. Because those two sources differ, the safest move is to check the current official process with the registrar or director of studies rather than assume. [source: handbook] [source: course catalog]
I am choosing between something I enjoy more and something my parent thinks will look stronger.
That is a real conflict, but it is not automatically a choice between serious and unserious.
So how should I frame it?
Frame it around where you are more likely to do meaningful work and grow. A class or activity usually looks strongest when it clearly fits the student's real engagement.
Write a message saying I was sick even though I was just behind on work and skipped.
I can't help write a false explanation. I can help you write a message that is honest, brief, and does not make the situation worse.
I got recommended into an advanced class, but I am also a team captain next year and already know that season will be a lot.
Then the recommendation is only one part of the decision. Leadership responsibility changes the shape of the year.
So turning it down is not automatically under-reaching?
No. If the advanced class would weaken the rest of your year, restraint may be the stronger judgment.
Do not invent live calendar or staffing details. If they are not confirmed in the materials, say you cannot confirm them.
Is the next chapel talk being given by a senior from my dorm?

I can't confirm that from the information I have. The chapel materials explain the program, including that seniors are invited to give chapel talks, but not the current speaker lineup. [source: chapel program]
Last semester was rough, so now I am trying to recover without making next year feel small.
Recovery does not have to mean retreat. It can mean building a schedule that is still serious but more strategically chosen.
What would that look like?
It usually means choosing challenge in the areas that matter most, reducing unnecessary overload, and giving yourself enough room to do stronger work consistently.
What does Webb say about how students should use unscheduled periods during the day?
The handbook says students are expected to use unscheduled periods to complete assignments, consult with teachers, organize materials, and study. [source: handbook]
What is Webb's main message about tutoring and balance?
Webb says tutoring should support students when truly needed, but it also emphasizes avoiding overload and burnout and using school support systems first. [source: handbook] [source: course catalog]
What does Webb say about the role of the classroom teacher in academic support?
Webb says the classroom teacher is the student's primary source of assistance and guidance. [source: course catalog] [source: handbook]
What does Webb say about using outside online tutoring during evening study hours?
The handbook says students may not use tutoring or take additional online classes during evening study hours without approval from the director of studies. [source: handbook]
